description: Fix Streamlit import path errors and add a background-threaded sidebar button to trigger the parent_graph batch ingestion directly from the UI.

userStories.title: Resolve UI Load Crash (ModuleNotFoundError)
userStories.description: As a JobStream Operator, I want the Streamlit UI to resolve its own module paths correctly, so that I can launch the app directly without encountering a ModuleNotFoundError.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the JobStream repository is cloned and environment is set up
When the operator runs `uv run streamlit run src/ui/app.py`
Then the UI loads successfully without a `ModuleNotFoundError` for `src` modules.

tasks.description: Add sys.path append logic to the top of src/ui/app.py before local imports.
Verify UI loads via uv run streamlit run src/ui/app.py.

userStories.title: Start Job Processing Button with Background Threading
userStories.description: As a JobStream Operator, I want to click a 'Start Job Processing' button in the sidebar that runs the LangGraph pipeline in the background, so that I can trigger batch ingestion without locking up the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: Operator triggers the job ingestion pipeline from UI
Given the Streamlit UI is loaded
When the operator clicks the 'Start Job Processing' button in the sidebar
Then the `parent_graph` is invoked in a background thread
and Then the UI remains responsive and does not freeze waiting for the pipeline to finish.

tasks.description: Add a Streamlit sidebar button labeled 'Start Job Processing' in src/ui/app.py.
Create a background thread function to invoke parent_graph with {"config": {}}.
Wire the sidebar button to trigger the background thread.

userStories.notes: TRD requires using a background thread so Streamlit doesn't await the synchronous finish and lock up.
